mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals household income expansion and consumption demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency is less than 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces unconventional easing and suppresses short-term discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) reveal a synchronized recovery in both output and final demand, amplifying earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where both INDPRO and RSAFS increase simultaneously fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the interaction term exhibits a t-statistic below 1.5 in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) lead positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because residential investment is an early-cycle driver that accelerates construction employment and durable goods demand, but this mechanism is only reliable during the first year of recovery from a deep contraction.
failure_condition: HOUST increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations during the 24 months following recession troughs, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% indicates severe excess capacity in labor markets that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, creating downward pressure on corporate earnings and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods when UNRATE exceeds 8%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Short-term changes in industrial production (INDPRO) capture contemporaneous manufacturing momentum that alters near-term earnings surprises, and positive surprises predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or hit rate of positive directional forecasts falls below 53%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an accommodative federal funds rate below 0.5% and month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a monetary-fueled labor market recovery that compresses the equity risk premium, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS fails to achieve a t-statistic above 1.5 in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals a sudden contraction in consumer expenditure that reduces expected revenues for discretionary and staple sectors, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines exceed 2%, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
